headline: Swiss-French border controls activated for June G7 window.
summary: The Federal Council confirmed temporary Schengen checks at all land and lake crossings with France (cantons Geneva, Vaud, Valais) from 10–19 June 2026, ahead of the G7 summit in Évian (15–17 June).

headline: June 14 referendums: population cap polling at statistical tie.
summary: A GfS Bern survey (field end May 3) shows 47%/47% on the SVP "No to 10 million" initiative; the civilian service reform proposal commands a narrow yes majority.

headline: Crans-Montana: Parmelin secures diplomatic de-escalation in Rome.
summary: Swiss President Guy Parmelin met Italian FM Tajani and President Mattarella on May 5, confirming Switzerland will absorb uninsured treatment costs for victims of the January 1 bar fire (41 dead) and halt the billing-copy practice that triggered Italy's ambassador recall in January.

headline: Bilateral III parliamentary deliberations under way; no vote date fixed.
summary: Both chambers are working through the four federal resolutions (stabilisation + electricity, health, food-safety agreements) submitted in March by the Federal Council.

headline: Vaud judiciary logs record caseload.
summary: Cantonal courts registered 63,799 new cases in 2025 (+4% YoY), with rising bankruptcies as key driver; figures from the 2025 management report published May 7.

headline: Trump-Xi Beijing summit: Taiwan warning dominates; trade yield thin.
summary: Day two ended with a "constructive, strategically stable" framework declaration; Xi declared Taiwan "the most important issue" and warned that mishandling it risks "conflict or collision" — language absent from the US readout, which focused on trade.

headline: Russia's largest aerial campaign since war's start hits Kyiv for third straight day.
summary: 1,560+ drones and 56 ballistic missiles fired since Wednesday morning; a Darnytsia apartment block partially collapsed (27 rescued from rubble); 9–17 killed across Ukraine, dozens injured.

headline: Iran-US: one-page MOU taking shape via Pakistani intermediaries.
summary: The memo would declare hostilities over and open a 30-day window for nuclear talks; core gap is the uranium enrichment moratorium duration — Iran proposed 5 years, US demands 20, with 12–15 years as reported landing zone.

headline: Modi departs on five-nation tour amid Hormuz energy stakes.
summary: PM departed May 15 for UAE (energy supply chains, diaspora), Netherlands, Sweden, Norway (first Indian PM visit in 40+ years), and Italy; India simultaneously hosting a BRICS meeting in New Delhi.

headline: JWST maps cosmic web to universe's first billion years.
summary: UC Riverside team analysed 164,000+ galaxies from the COSMOS-Web survey to trace large-scale filament structure back to when the universe was one billion years old — an era "essentially out of reach before JWST." Published in *The Astrophysical Journal*, May 6 2026.

headline: Four-week diet shift improves biological age in older-adult RCT (*Aging Cell*).
summary: University of Sydney RCT (104 adults, ages 65–75, four diet arms varying fat level and plant-vs.-animal protein ratio) found low-fat and plant-heavy diets measurably improved 20-biomarker biological-age scores within four weeks.

headline: McGill identifies brown-fat thermogenesis molecular switch (*Nature*).
summary: Lawrence Kazak's group at McGill's Goodman Cancer Institute found that glycerol binds to enzyme TNAP's "glycerol pocket," activating the futile creatine cycle — an alternative heat-producing pathway in brown adipose tissue.

headline: Kyoto University: real-time detection of quantum W states.
summary: New photonic method enables instant identification of tripartite entangled W states, removing the post-processing bottleneck for quantum communication and teleportation networks; prior methods required offline analysis.

headline: arXiv:2605.00419
summary: (~May 1–2 2026) [preprint] [via snippet] — *"Rethinking LLM Ensembling from the Perspective of Mixture Models"* — Reframes ensemble as stochastic single-model token-step selection; 1.78×–2.68× speedup over conventional ensemble inference with competitive quality.

headline: arXiv:2605.07501
summary: (~May 8 2026) [preprint] [via snippet] — *"ExpThink: Experience-Guided Reinforcement Learning for Adaptive Chain-of-Thought Compression"* — RL-based method to selectively compress chain-of-thought traces, cutting token consumption and inference latency without quality collapse.

headline: arXiv:2605.10648
summary: (~May 10–11 2026) [preprint] [via snippet] — *"Demystifying Deep Reinforcement Learning: A Neuro-Symbolic Framework for Interpretable Open RAN Automation"* — Distils black-box DRL policies into compact human-readable symbolic representations for deterministic, low-latency inference; interpretability

headline: arXiv:2605.12653
summary: (~May 12 2026) [preprint] [via snippet] — *"Plan Before You Trade: Inference-Time Optimization for RL Trading Agents"* — FPILOT plugs an MPC-inspired forecaster into RL policy inference; uses imagined price-trajectory returns to optimize at test time without retraining.

headline: Asian close (May 15):
summary: Nikkei 225 −0.9%, Hang Seng −0.39%, CSI 300 roughly flat.

headline: EUR/CHF 0.9150 · USD/CHF 0.7829 (May 14 close).
summary: ECB FX XML feed unavailable (HTTP 403); figures from snippet aggregators.

headline: US equity futures: slight upward bias.
summary: S&P 500 futures ~7,521; Dow ~50,109 pre-open, following May 14 records (S&P +0.77% to 7,501; Dow retook 50,000; Nasdaq +0.88% to 26,635).
